findings.title: Hard-coded worker log location duplicates configurable logging knowledge
findings.impact: The new command bakes in /opt/yuanrong/datasystem/log and later constructs <address>.log, while worker startup and the logging subsystem already treat log_dir and log_filename as configurable. Future logging layout changes now need to be remembered in this CLI command as well, and the comments/spec can mislead maintainers into thinking this is the canonical worker log path.
findings.fix: Resolve the log directory and filename from the same worker configuration/command-line flags used by start/logging, or factor a small CLI helper that derives the active worker log path from existing worker config defaults instead of hard-coding a parallel convention.

findings.title: Worker process discovery is copied instead of shared with stop
findings.impact: find_worker_pid reimplements the same pgrep-based -worker_address= scan described as mirroring stop.py, but with different filtering and error behavior. That leaves two places to update when worker invocation, pgrep options, or dscli self-filtering changes, and makes the subtle differences look accidental rather than intentional.
findings.fix: Move the common worker-address-to-PID scan into a shared CLI helper (or reuse stop.Command's helper after extracting the non-stop-specific parts) and let logs choose whether zero/multiple matches are warnings or failures at its call site.
